prompt: Draft a detailed surface plasmon resonance (SPR) protocol for measuring the binding kinetics of a neutralising antibody to protective antigen PA83 from Bacillus anthracis as part of an antitoxin development and quality control programme. Recombinant PA83 is produced from E. coli without lethal factor or edema factor and is not toxic at BSL-1 concentrations. The protocol should include: (1) CM5 chip preparation: amine coupling of recombinant PA83 (target 150 RU), (2) reference channel preparation with ethanolamine blocking, (3) antibody analyte preparation: 0.5–50 nM serial dilution in PBS-T, (4) single-cycle kinetics: 120 s association, 600 s dissociation, (5) regeneration: 10 mM glycine pH 2.0 for 30 s × 3 cycles, (6) data fitting with 1:1 Langmuir model and bulk refractive index correction, (7) acceptance criteria and antibody potency threshold (KD < 1 nM for neutralisation). Include a biosafety note on handling recombinant PA83 (non-toxic, BSL-1).
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and anthrax antitoxin development literature; target: PA83; organism: Bacillus anthracis; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_name: Anti-anthrax PA neutralising antibody SPR assay
_provenance.target_class: select agent toxin component neutralising antibody binding characterisation
